title: Integrity Forensics Report
description: The synthesized output of a /anti-autoresearch run. Produced by tools/adjudicate_findings.py (deterministic) from the union of all skills' findings. The overall_verdict is computed by RULES, not by an LLM — this is the structural defense against 'LLM slop grading LLM slop'.

taxonomy_version.description: Version of references/hack-pattern-taxonomy.md used — recorded so a report is interpretable after the taxonomy evolves.

overall_verdict.description: Deliberately NOT 'fabricated/honest'. CLEAN_GIVEN_EVIDENCE = no flags at the available observability level (NOT 'the paper is honest'). SOFT_FLAGS = minor/medium-FP-risk discrepancies a reviewer should ask about. HARD_FLAGS = ≥1 span-anchored critical discrepancy decidable at this observability level.

adjudicator.description: e.g. "deterministic-rules-v0" — the rule engine + version, never an LLM identity.

adversarial_memo.description: Evidence-bound rejection memo from adversarial-case-builder. Informational; contributes NO verdict weight.

downgraded_for_observability.description: How many findings the adjudicator demoted because the run's evidence tier was below the finding's required level.

limitations.description: What this run could NOT check (always populated — honesty is part of the contract).

human_review_required.description: Always true. The report is decision SUPPORT for a human reviewer/AC; it never issues a verdict on misconduct.
